Texas · Border · 2020 ACS & CGD

Colonias Dashboard

Demographic, economic and infrastructure profiles for 1,716 Texas colonias along the Rio Grande.

About the colonias

Tiny border towns on the edge of Texas — unincorporated neighborhoods where families have built homes, schools, and futures over decades.

What is a colonia?

A colonia (co·LOH·nee·ah) is a small, residential community along the Texas–Mexico border. Many began as unplatted land sold lot-by-lot to working families, often without paved roads, piped water, or a sewer hookup. The state of Texas tracks 1,716 of them.

●  Black dots on the map mark major border cities — click one for its city hall.
